# "Do Nothing" Competitive Profile



## What "Do Nothing" Is



The most common competitor Halosight faces is not a software platform. It is the existing behavior of field reps manually documenting (or not documenting) customer interactions through habit, memory, and delayed CRM entry.



"Do nothing" means the organization is aware of the problem but has not assigned urgency, budget, or ownership to solving it.



---



## How "Do Nothing" Currently Works



In most field sales organizations without Halosight:



- Reps visit customers throughout the day

- Notes are taken mentally, on paper, or in a personal notes app

- CRM updates happen that evening, the next morning, or not at all

- Intelligence quality degrades with time and memory

- Commitments are tracked in the rep's head or a personal system

- Managers depend on rep interpretation during pipeline reviews

- Salesforce data reflects what reps chose to enter, not what actually happened



---



## Why "Do Nothing" Feels Safe to the Prospect



- No new budget required

- No implementation risk

- No change management required

- The problem feels manageable because it has always existed

- Leadership has adapted to working around unreliable CRM data



---



## Why "Do Nothing" Is Actually Costly



### Revenue Leakage

Commitments made in customer meetings are forgotten or deprioritized. Follow-through gaps create lost orders and customer experience failures.



### CRM Data Decay

Every day a rep's visit goes unlogged is a day of intelligence lost. Over a quarter, the CRM reflects a fraction of actual field activity.



### Management Blind Spots

Pipeline reviews are based on rep interpretation, not documented customer intelligence. Managers cannot inspect what they cannot see.



### Onboarding Inefficiency

When a rep leaves or a territory changes, the customer intelligence leaves with them. There is no organizational memory.



### Coaching Without Evidence

Managers cannot coach on execution quality they have never observed. Coaching defaults to results-based feedback rather than behavior-based improvement.



---



## Creating Urgency Against "Do Nothing"



Strong urgency triggers to surface:



- "How many customer visits did your field team complete last quarter? How many of those are documented in Salesforce?"

- "When a rep leaves, what happens to the customer relationships they managed?"

- "How confident are you in your pipeline forecast given the documentation quality of your field team?"

- "When was the last time a commitment was missed because it wasn't documented?"

- "If you scaled your field team by 20%, would your current documentation process scale with it?"



---



## The Cost of Inaction Frame



The cost of "do nothing" is not a software cost — it is an operational cost the organization is already paying:



- Lost orders from missed follow-through

- Management time spent reconstructing what happened in deals

- Forecast inaccuracy causing missed quarters

- Onboarding delays when territory knowledge lives in rep memory

- Coaching inefficiency from lack of observable execution data



---



## Recommended Reframe



*"You're not really deciding between Halosight and another tool. You're deciding whether the current approach — reps documenting from memory hours or days later — is good enough for where you're trying to take this organization. It's worked until now. The question is whether it scales."*



---

# Alysio Competitive Profile



## What Alysio Is



Alysio is a sales performance and rep coaching platform focused on activity tracking, goal management, and performance visibility. It helps sales managers set rep targets, track activity metrics, and run structured coaching conversations based on performance data.



Alysio is designed to improve rep accountability and management coaching cadence through structured performance management workflows.



---



## Where Alysio Plays



- Sales organizations focused on rep activity accountability

- Teams wanting structured coaching cadences tied to performance metrics

- Managers seeking visibility into rep goal attainment and activity volume

- Organizations running formal performance improvement workflows



---



## Where Alysio Does NOT Play



- Customer interaction intelligence capture

- AI-structured post-meeting summaries

- CRM population from field activity

- Voice-based mobile capture for field reps

- Salesforce field population from interaction data



---



## The Fundamental Difference



Alysio measures **how much** reps are doing — activity volume, goal attainment, coaching completion rates.



Halosight captures **what is actually happening** in those activities — the substance of customer interactions, what was committed, what intelligence was gathered, and how it flows into Salesforce.



Activity volume without interaction quality is a management blind spot. Halosight addresses what Alysio cannot see.



---



## Core Capability Gaps vs. Halosight



### Interaction Intelligence

Alysio tracks that a rep made 8 customer visits today. Halosight captures what happened in each of those visits — customer sentiment, commitments made, next steps agreed, competitive intelligence surfaced, and order discussions initiated.



### CRM Data Quality

Alysio does not improve the quality of CRM data. Halosight directly writes structured interaction intelligence to Salesforce fields, improving CRM reliability at the source.



### Field Rep Mobile Workflow

Alysio is a performance management platform, not a field capture tool. It has no voice memo capability or AI structuring of in-person customer interactions.



### Execution Visibility vs. Activity Visibility

Alysio tells managers whether reps are hitting activity targets. Halosight tells managers whether the execution quality inside those activities is sound — whether the right questions were asked, whether commitments were captured, and whether the opportunity is healthy.



---



## Most Common Competitive Situation



Alysio may already be deployed for performance management purposes. It is not a replacement situation — it is a gap identification situation.



**Positioning: Complementary — Alysio measures activity volume, Halosight captures execution quality.**



---



## Recommended Reframe



*"Alysio helps you track whether reps are hitting their activity numbers — that's important. But activity volume doesn't tell you what's happening inside those interactions. Halosight gives you the execution intelligence — what was discussed, what was committed, and whether it made it into Salesforce. You need both."*

- "When a rep completes their 8 visits today, what visibility do you have into what actually happened in each one?"

- "If a rep is hitting their call numbers but deals aren't moving, how do you diagnose what's happening?"

# Competitive Intelligence Overview



## Competitive Landscape Summary



Halosight operates in the AI Sales Enablement space with a specific focus on field and outside sales execution. No single competitor replicates the full Halosight workflow. Competitive overlap is partial and category-specific.



---



## Competitive Categories



### Category 1 — Sales Content & Enablement Platforms

These tools focus on delivering content to reps before and during customer interactions. They do not capture execution intelligence after the meeting.



- Showell Competitive Profile

- Showpad Competitive Profile

- Seismic Competitive Profile



**Core Differentiation:** These platforms manage what reps bring into a meeting. Halosight manages what comes out of it — structured execution intelligence, CRM population, and management visibility.



---



### Category 2 — Performance & Coaching Platforms

These tools focus on rep activity metrics, goal tracking, and performance management. They measure behavior volume but not customer interaction quality.



- Alysio Competitive Profile



**Core Differentiation:** Alysio tracks rep activity counts. Halosight captures the substance and quality of those activities and structures them into operational intelligence.



---



### Category 3 — Conversation Intelligence Platforms

These tools record and analyze sales calls, primarily for inside sales teams using video or phone. They are not designed for field sales workflows.



- Gong Competitive Profile

- Chorus Competitive Profile



**Core Differentiation:** Gong and Chorus require a screen, a camera, or a phone line. Halosight is built for reps in trucks, warehouses, and customer facilities — mobile-first, voice-first, field-first.



---



## Universal Competitive Principle



Halosight wins when the conversation is about:

- **field sales execution** — no competitor owns this space

- **mobile-first CRM population** — competitors require desk-based workflows

- **post-meeting intelligence capture** — content tools don't do this at all

- **Salesforce data quality** — Halosight writes structured data directly to Salesforce fields instantly



Halosight loses or stalls when positioned as:

- a generic AI tool

- a note-taking upgrade

- a replacement for tools already deployed



---



## Additive vs. Replacement Framing



For organizations already using Gong or Chorus for inside sales:

- Position Halosight as **additive**, not competitive

- Gong/Chorus covers the conference room and Zoom call

- Halosight covers the parking lot, the warehouse floor, and the customer site visit

- Together they create full-spectrum sales intelligence

## What Seismic Is

Seismic is typically deployed in large enterprise environments with significant content management challenges.

Seismic is a content intelligence platform. It is designed to optimize what reps use to engage buyers — materials, presentations, proposals.



Halosight is an execution intelligence platform. It is designed to capture, structure, and operationalize what actually happens in customer interactions — and push that intelligence into Salesforce immediately.



---



## Core Capability Gaps vs. Halosight



### Post-Meeting Intelligence

Seismic does not capture or structure what happened in a customer meeting. Its visibility ends at content delivery.



### Field Sales Workflow

Seismic is primarily a desktop and browser platform designed for enterprise sales teams with structured pre-meeting workflows. It is not built for a field rep recording a voice memo between customer stops.



### Instant CRM Population

Seismic does not write structured customer interaction data to Salesforce fields. Halosight does this automatically at the conclusion of every recorded interaction.

Seismic tells managers what content was used. Halosight tells managers what the rep committed to, what the customer said, and what the next steps are — structured and in Salesforce.

*"Seismic is solving a content and enablement problem — making sure reps have what they need going into meetings. title: Showpad Competitive Profile

# Showpad Competitive Profile



## What Showpad Is



Showpad is a sales enablement platform focused on content management, delivery, and training at scale. It helps organizations manage approved sales materials, deliver onboarding and certification programs, and provide visibility into content engagement across sales teams.



Showpad's core value is organizational consistency — ensuring reps across a distributed team have the right materials, messaging, and training to engage buyers effectively.



---



## Competitive Classification



**Showpad is not a competitor. It is a natural integration partner.**



Halosight and Showpad operate at different layers of the field sales workflow and create a reinforcing feedback loop when used together.



---



## How the Layers Differ



### Halosight — The Execution Layer

Halosight operates at the moment of selling. It captures what happens in individual customer interactions, structures that intelligence, pushes it to Salesforce, and provides micro-coaching to the rep after every meeting.



Halosight answers: *"What just happened — what needs to happen next — and how can the next meeting be slightly better?"*



### Showpad — The Enablement Layer

Showpad operates at the organizational level. It scales knowledge, messaging, training, and content governance across the sales organization.



Showpad answers: *"How do we consistently equip reps with the right materials and guidance?"*



---



## The Feedback Loop They Create Together



**After every meeting:**

- Halosight captures what happened and delivers micro-coaching to the rep

- Reps receive immediate reinforcement, clearer next steps, and reduced admin burden

- Customer commitments and outcomes flow into Salesforce more consistently



**Over time:**

- Patterns emerge across conversations — common objections, recurring questions, execution gaps, follow-through failures

- These patterns inform coaching priorities and enablement focus



**At scale:**

- Showpad operationalizes those priorities through content, training, and playbooks

- Reps improve both in the moment (Halosight) and over the long term (Showpad)



---



## Core Capability Differences



| Capability | Halosight | Showpad |

|---|---|---|

| Post-meeting intelligence capture | ✅ | ❌ |

| AI-structured interaction summaries | ✅ | ❌ |

| Salesforce field population | ✅ | ❌ |

| Micro-coaching after every interaction | ✅ | ❌ |

| Management visibility into field execution | ✅ | Partial |

| Content management and delivery | ❌ | ✅ |

| Onboarding and certification programs | ❌ | ✅ |

| Scaled enablement and playbooks | ❌ | ✅ |

| Content engagement analytics | ❌ | ✅ |



---



## What This Means for Reps



Together, Halosight and Showpad provide:

- Support without surveillance

- Coaching without extra meetings

- Clear follow-through without extra admin

- A more unified workflow around customer interactions

- Materials to act on coaching guidance (Showpad) grounded in what's actually happening (Halosight)



---



## What This Means for Leaders



Together, Halosight and Showpad provide:

- Visibility into customer experience without shadowing or surveys

- More accurate CRM activity and account context

- Rep development grounded in real interactions, not assumptions

- Enablement priorities based on what's actually happening in the field — not assumptions



---



## Positioning in Sales Conversations



If a prospect uses Showpad:

- Do not position Halosight as competitive

- Position as the missing execution layer — Showpad handles what goes in, Halosight handles what comes out

- The combination creates a closed-loop system: field reality informs enablement, enablement improves field execution



If a prospect is evaluating both:

- Lead with the integration narrative

- Halosight and Showpad serve the same field sales customer at different workflow moments



---



## Recommended Reframe



*"Showpad helps your reps show up with the right materials and training — that's the organizational consistency layer. Halosight captures what actually happens in those meetings and feeds that intelligence back into Salesforce and to the rep immediately. Over time, the patterns Halosight surfaces inform exactly what Showpad prioritizes in coaching and content. They're built for the same field rep — just at different moments."*



---

title: Deal Inspection Prompt
content:
# Deal Inspection Prompt



## When to Use



During weekly pipeline reviews or before a 1:1 with a rep on a specific deal. Designed for sales managers who want a structured assessment of deal health based on documented activity.



## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Pipeline Inspection

- Deal Risk Indicators

- Sales Stages

- Qualification Standards



---



## The Prompt



```

I need to inspect this deal before my pipeline review.



Account: [Company Name]

Rep: [Rep Name]

Deal Stage: [Current sales stage]

Deal Size: [Estimated contract value]

Close Date: [Projected close date]



What I know about this deal:

- Key stakeholders identified: [List names and titles]

- Economic buyer confirmed: [Yes / No / Uncertain]

- Problem clearly defined: [What problem are they solving with Halosight?]

- Last meaningful interaction: [Date and what happened]

- Next scheduled step: [What's booked and when]

- Known risks or concerns: [Anything that could stall or kill this deal]

- Rep's stated confidence: [How confident is the rep and why]



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me:



1. DEAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT — Is this deal on track, at risk, or stalled? Why?

2. MISSING INFORMATION — What critical qualification information is absent from this deal?

3. PRIMARY RISK — What is the single biggest risk to this deal closing on time?

4. INSPECTION QUESTIONS — 3 questions I should ask the rep in our next 1:1 to get a clearer picture

5. RECOMMENDED ACTION — What is the single most important thing the rep should do in the next 7 days to advance this deal?

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompt



```

I need to inspect this deal before my pipeline review.



Account: Southern Industrial Supply

Rep: Marcus Webb

Deal Stage: Evaluation / Demo Completed

Deal Size: $42,000 annual

Close Date: End of quarter (45 days out)



What I know about this deal:

- Key stakeholders identified: VP of Sales (Tom Garrett), IT Director (unknown name)

- Economic buyer confirmed: Uncertain — Tom seems interested but hasn't confirmed budget authority

- Problem clearly defined: Field reps documenting inconsistently, Salesforce data unreliable

- Last meaningful interaction: Demo 2 weeks ago — went well per Marcus

- Next scheduled step: Nothing booked — Marcus said Tom is "thinking it over"

- Known risks or concerns: IT Director hasn't been in any conversation yet. No next step booked.

- Rep's stated confidence: 70% — Marcus thinks Tom wants it but isn't sure about internal approval



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return:

- An honest deal health assessment — not optimistic by default

- A specific list of what's missing (economic buyer confirmation, IT involvement, next step, etc.)

- The primary risk named clearly (in the example: no next step + IT not engaged = stall risk)

- Three sharp inspection questions the manager can ask the rep

- One specific action recommendation with a timeframe



---



## Tips



- "Rep's stated confidence" is often the most important field — Claude will flag when rep confidence is disconnected from deal reality

- If "Next scheduled step" is blank or vague, that's almost always the primary risk

- Use this prompt for every deal over a certain size threshold — not just troubled deals



---



## Related Prompts



- Rep Coaching Brief Prompt — use after deal inspection to prepare for the coaching conversation

- Post-Visit Debrief Prompt — rep-facing version for individual visit documentation



---



## Status



Approved — Ready for Manager Use

title: Discovery Call Planner Prompt
content:
# Discovery Call Planner Prompt



## When to Use



Before a first discovery call or meeting with a new prospect. Use this to build a tailored discovery plan rather than running a generic script.



## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Discovery Framework

- Ideal Customer Profile

- Relevant persona files (load all if stakeholders are unknown)

- Canonical Operational Problems



---



## The Prompt



```

I have a discovery call with [Company Name] on [date/time].



What I know about them:

- Industry: [e.g. automotive parts distribution, industrial supplies, construction materials]

- Company size: [approximate headcount or revenue if known]

- Field sales team size: [number of field reps if known, or "unknown"]

- Who I'm meeting: [Names and titles — list all if multiple stakeholders]

- How they found us / why they're talking to us: [referral, outbound, inbound, event, etc.]

- Anything I already know about their situation: [any intel from LinkedIn, previous calls, referral context]



Using the Halosight knowledge base, build me a discovery plan with:



1. OPENING — A 2-sentence framing statement to open the call that establishes why we're here without pitching

2. PRIMARY DISCOVERY QUESTIONS — 5 questions tailored to their industry and the roles I'm meeting, ordered from broad to specific

3. QUALIFICATION SIGNALS — 3 things I should listen for that confirm this is a strong ICP fit

4. DISQUALIFICATION SIGNALS — 2 things I should listen for that suggest this may not be the right fit right now

5. PROOF POINT TO HAVE READY — Which customer result should I have ready if they ask for proof?

6. SUGGESTED CLOSE — How should I close this discovery call to secure a clear next step?

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompt



```

I have a discovery call with National Parts Wholesale on Thursday at 2pm.



What I know about them:

- Industry: Automotive aftermarket parts distribution

- Company size: ~$180M revenue, regional distributor

- Field sales team size: Approximately 20 field reps across 3 states

- Who I'm meeting: Director of Sales Operations and their VP of Sales

- How they found us: Referral from an industry contact

- Anything I already know: The person who referred us mentioned they're frustrated with Salesforce adoption and that their VP of Sales is under pressure to improve forecasting accuracy



Using the Halosight knowledge base, build me a discovery plan...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return:

- A natural, non-salesy opening framing statement

- Five sharp discovery questions that don't feel like an interrogation

- Specific qualification signals tied to Halosight's ICP (field team size, CRM problems, management visibility gaps)

- Honest disqualification signals (e.g. inside-only sales team, no Salesforce, very small team)

- The right proof point for this industry and these roles

- A closing question or statement that creates a clear next step



---



## Tips



- The more context you provide about why they're talking to you, the more targeted the questions

- If you're meeting multiple stakeholders, note each person's title — Claude will calibrate questions for the room

- Use this in combination with the Pre-Visit Prep Prompt if the discovery is in person



---



## Related Prompts



- Pre-Visit Prep Prompt — if discovery is in person

- Stakeholder Mapping Prompt — if multiple stakeholders are involved

- Post-Visit Debrief Prompt — immediately after the call



---

Approved — Ready for Field Use

title: Follow-Up Email Prompt
content:
# Follow-Up Email Prompt



## When to Use



Within 1 hour of leaving a customer visit or discovery call. The faster this goes out, the stronger the impression.



## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Approved Language

- Controlled Vocabulary



---



## The Prompt



```

I need a follow-up email after my visit with [Customer/Prospect Name].



Who I'm emailing: [Name, Title]

Visit context: [First visit / follow-up / existing customer]

Key things discussed: [2-4 bullet points of what was covered]

Commitments I made: [What did you promise to send or do?]

Commitments they made: [What did they agree to do next?]

Desired next step: [What do you want them to do after reading this email?]

Tone: [Formal / conversational — default to conversational for field sales]



Write a follow-up email using Halosight's approved messaging and language. The email should:

- Open with a specific reference to something real from the conversation (not a generic "great meeting you")

- Recap the key points discussed in 2-3 sentences

- Confirm any commitments made on both sides

- Include a clear, single call to action

- Be 150 words or less — field sales buyers don't read long emails



Do not use buzzwords or AI-sounding language. Write like a real person.

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompt



```

I need a follow-up email after my visit with Coastal Auto Distributors.



Who I'm emailing: Sarah Kowalski, VP of Sales

Visit context: First visit

Key things discussed:

- Their field reps visit 50+ accounts per month but CRM updates happen inconsistently

- Leadership has no real visibility into what reps are discussing with customers

- Sarah mentioned they lost a key account last quarter because a commitment was missed

- She was interested when I described how Halosight writes directly to Salesforce fields



Commitments I made: Send her the Advance Auto Parts results one-pager

Commitments they made: Sarah said she'd think about whether to include their IT director in a next conversation

Desired next step: Schedule a 30-minute demo with Sarah and potentially IT

Tone: Conversational



Write a follow-up email using Halosight's approved messaging...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return a complete, ready-to-send email with:

- A subject line

- A specific, human opening line

- A crisp recap of the visit

- Confirmed next steps from both sides

- A single clear call to action

- 150 words or less



---



## Tips



- The more specific your "key things discussed" bullets, the more personalized the email

- If the prospect said something memorable, include it in quotes — Claude will reference it naturally

- "Do not use buzzwords" is important — review the output before sending and remove any AI-sounding phrases



---



## Related Prompts



- Post-Visit Debrief Prompt — run this first to organize your visit notes

- Objection Response Prompt — if they raised an objection you need to address in the email



---

Approved — Ready for Field Use

title: New Rep Orientation Prompt
content:
# New Rep Orientation Prompt



## When to Use



During a new rep's first week. Gives them a structured orientation to Halosight's positioning, ICP, competitive landscape, and sales approach — without requiring them to read the entire vault manually.



## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Canonical Company Definition

- Canonical Positioning

- Ideal Customer Profile

- All persona files

- Competitive Intelligence Overview

- Discovery Framework

- Objection Handling

- Advance Auto Parts Source Evidence



---



## The Prompt



```

I'm a new sales rep at Halosight. I'm starting my first week and need to get up to speed quickly.



My background: [e.g. "I come from 3 years of field sales at a manufacturing distributor" OR "I'm new to field sales — came from inside sales"]

My territory: [Geographic region or account type if known]

My biggest knowledge gap right now: [e.g. "I don't fully understand how Halosight is different from Gong" OR "I'm not sure how to explain what we do in simple terms" OR "I don't know how to handle the 'reps won't use it' objection"]



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me:



1. THE 60-SECOND EXPLANATION — How do I explain what Halosight does to someone who has never heard of it? Write it the way I'd actually say it, not as a corporate definition.

2. WHO I'M SELLING TO — Describe my ideal customer in plain language. What kind of company, what kind of people, what problem are they experiencing?

3. THE THREE MOST COMMON OBJECTIONS — What will prospects say most often, and what's the right response to each?

4. OUR BEST PROOF — What is our strongest customer evidence and how do I use it in a conversation?

5. WHAT MAKES US DIFFERENT — How do I explain why Halosight is different from tools they may already have (especially Gong)?

6. MY FIRST PRIORITY — What should I focus on learning or doing in my first 30 days to be effective as quickly as possible?

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompt



```

I'm a new sales rep at Halosight. I'm starting my first week and need to get up to speed quickly.



My background: 4 years of outside sales at an industrial fastener distributor — I know the field sales world but I've never sold software before

My territory: Midwest — primarily industrial distribution and automotive aftermarket

My biggest knowledge gap right now: I'm not sure how to explain what Halosight does without it sounding complicated or tech-heavy



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return:

- A plain-language 60-second explanation a new rep can memorize and say naturally

- A clear, simple ICP description without jargon

- Three objections with natural-sounding responses, not scripts

- The Advance Auto Parts proof point formatted for verbal use

- A crisp differentiation statement vs. Gong/Chorus

- A practical 30-day focus recommendation



---



## Tips



- The "biggest knowledge gap" field is critical — it focuses Claude on what the rep actually needs rather than covering everything

- New reps with field sales backgrounds need less industry context and more product/positioning context

- New reps from inside sales backgrounds need more field sales workflow context

- Run this prompt multiple times with different "biggest knowledge gap" inputs to cover different areas



---



## Variations



**For a rep inheriting an existing territory:**

Use the Territory Handoff Brief Prompt instead — it focuses on account-specific context rather than general orientation.



**For a manager onboarding a new rep:**

Run this prompt yourself first, then share the output as a study guide before the rep's first week.



---



## Related Prompts



- Territory Handoff Brief Prompt — for account-specific onboarding context

- Pre-Visit Prep Prompt — once the rep is ready for their first customer visits

- Discovery Call Planner Prompt — once the rep is ready for first discovery calls



---

Approved — Ready for Onboarding Use

title: Objection Response Prompt
content:
# Objection Response Prompt



## When to Use



When a prospect or customer raises a specific objection and you want a grounded, vault-aligned response — either to use in the moment or to prepare for a follow-up conversation.



## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Objection Handling

- What We Are Not

- Relevant competitive profile (if objection involves a competitor)

- Advance Auto Parts Source Evidence (for proof-based responses)



---



## The Prompt



```

A prospect just raised this objection: "[paste the exact objection here]"



Context:

- Who said it: [Title/role, e.g. VP of Sales, IT Director, CRO]

- Where we are in the process: [First visit / mid-evaluation / close]

- What we had just discussed before they said it: [Brief context]

- Their tone: [Skeptical / genuinely curious / resistant / just asking]



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me:



1. WHAT THIS OBJECTION REALLY MEANS — What is the underlying concern behind this objection?

2. THE REFRAME — How should I reframe this in 2-3 sentences?

3. THE RESPONSE — A specific, natural-sounding verbal response I can use

4. THE FOLLOW-UP QUESTION — One question I should ask after my response to advance the conversation

5. PROOF POINT — Is there a customer result or evidence I should reference here?



Write the verbal response the way a confident, experienced rep would actually say it — not like a scripted pitch.

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompts



### Example 1 — Rep Adoption Objection

```

A prospect just raised this objection: "My reps won't use another tool. We've tried things before and adoption always fails."



Context:

- Who said it: VP of Sales

- Where we are in the process: Second visit, mid-evaluation

- What we had just discussed: I had just shown them the mobile recording workflow

- Their tone: Skeptical but not hostile — they've been burned before



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me...

```



### Example 2 — Competitor Objection

```

A prospect just raised this objection: "We already have Gong. Why would we need something else?"



Context:

- Who said it: CRO

- Where we are in the process: First discovery conversation

- What we had just discussed: I asked about their field sales team coverage

- Their tone: Genuinely curious, not defensive



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me...

```



### Example 3 — ROI Objection

```

A prospect just raised this objection: "I'm not sure we can justify the cost right now."



Context:

- Who said it: VP of Sales Ops

- Where we are in the process: Late stage — they've seen the demo

- What we had just discussed: Pricing conversation

- Their tone: Not resistant, seems like they want help making the case internally



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return:

- A clear diagnosis of the real underlying concern

- A reframe that doesn't dismiss the objection but redirects it productively

- A natural verbal response (not a bullet list — actual sentences you'd say out loud)

- A follow-up question that keeps the conversation moving

- A specific proof point if one exists



---



## Tips



- Paste the exact words the prospect used if you can — it helps Claude calibrate the response

- The "tone" field matters significantly — a resistant IT Director needs a different response than a curious VP of Sales

- Review the verbal response out loud before using it — adjust any language that doesn't sound like you



---



## Related Prompts



- Pre-Visit Prep Prompt — anticipate objections before they arise

- Deal Inspection Prompt — for manager-level objection pattern analysis



---



## Status



Approved — Ready for Field Use

title: Post-Call Debrief Prompt
content:
# Post-Call Debrief Prompt



## When to Use



Immediately after a customer or prospect Call. Designed to work with a voice-to-text transcription or quick typed notes.



## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Sales Stages

- Deal Risk Indicators

- Objection Handling



---



## The Prompt



```

I just finished a Call with [Customer/Prospect Name]. Here's what happened:



Who I met with: [Name and title if known]

Visit type: [First visit / follow-up / existing customer check-in]

What we discussed: [Paste your Halosight voice summary here, OR type a quick recap — even bullet points work]



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me:



1. NEXT STEP — The single most important thing I need to do before my next visit with this account, and when I should do it

2. COMMITMENT CHECK — Any commitment I made that I need to follow through on

3. OPPORTUNITY SIGNAL — Did anything in this visit suggest a Halosight opportunity or expansion? If yes, what is it?

4. RISK FLAG — Is there anything from this visit that puts this deal or relationship at risk?

5. ONE-LINE MANAGER SUMMARY — A single sentence I could send my manager right now that captures the outcome of this visit



Keep it brief.

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompt



```

I just finished a call with Tri-State Fastener Supply. Here's what happened:



Who I met with: Tom Hendricks, VP of Sales

Visit type: Second visit — follow-up from discovery two weeks ago

What we discussed: Tom said his reps are visiting 8-10 accounts per day but Salesforce is only getting updated maybe 40% of the time. He's frustrated because he can't trust his pipeline numbers. He asked about pricing and whether we integrate with their Salesforce instance. I told him we do and that I'd send him integration details. He seemed interested but said he'd need to loop in their IT director before moving forward.



Using the Halosight knowledge base, give me...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return:

- A specific, time-bound next step (e.g. "Send Salesforce integration one-pager to Tom today, and propose a 30-minute call with Tom and IT director by end of week")

- Any explicit commitment flagged (e.g. "You committed to sending integration details")

- An opportunity signal if present (e.g. "Tom's 40% CRM adoption rate is a strong Halosight fit signal")

- A risk flag if present (e.g. "IT director involvement could stall — address proactively")

- A crisp one-line summary for manager communication



---



## Tips



- If you used Halosight to record the visit, paste the AI summary directly into "What we discussed"

- If you didn't record, even 3-4 bullet points of what happened is enough

- The ONE-LINE MANAGER SUMMARY field is useful for quick Slack or text updates to your manager



---



## Related Prompts



- Pre-Visit Prep Prompt — use before the next visit to this account

- Follow-Up Email Prompt — use to draft the follow-up email from this visit

- Deal Inspection Prompt — manager version for deeper deal review



---



## Status



Approved — Ready for Rep Use

title: Pre-Visit Prep Prompt
content:
# Pre-Visit Prep Prompt



## When to Use



At Least 2 hours prior to schedule prospect/customer call.


## Vault Files to Load



- Halosight Core Context Pack

- Ideal Customer Profile

- Discovery Framework

- Relevant persona file (CRO, VP Sales, etc. if known)



---



## The Prompt



```

I'm about to have a discussion with [Customer/Prospect Name] in [X minutes/today].



Here's what I know about them:

- Industry: [e.g. automotive distribution, industrial supplies]

- Role of person I'm meeting: [e.g. VP of Sales, branch manager, owner]

- Current situation: [e.g. existing customer, first visit, follow-up from last week]

- Last interaction: [e.g. "Last visit we discussed pricing on SKU X" or "First time visiting this account"]

- Known issues or opportunities: [e.g. "They mentioned CRM adoption is a problem" or "Not sure yet"]



Using the Halosight knowledge base, help me prepare for this visit. Give me:



1. THREE opening questions I should ask based on their role and situation

2. ONE problem statement I should listen for that would signal strong Halosight fit

3. TWO things I should be ready to explain if they ask what Halosight does

4. Any relevant customer proof point I should have ready (e.g. Advance Auto Parts results)



Keep each answer to 2-3 sentences maximum. I need to read this before I walk in.

```



---



## Example Filled-In Prompt



```

I'm about to speak with Midwest Bearing Supply in 20 minutes.



Here's what I know about them:

- Industry: Industrial bearing and power transmission distribution

- Role of person I'm meeting: VP of Sales

- Current situation: First visit, came through a referral

- Last interaction: Brief 10-minute phone call last week — they mentioned reps are bad at updating Salesforce

- Known issues or opportunities: CRM adoption problem, 12-person field team



Using the Halosight knowledge base, help me prepare for this visit...

```



---



## What Good Output Looks Like



Claude should return:

- Three sharp, open-ended discovery questions tailored to a VP of Sales in distribution

- A clear problem signal to listen for (e.g. "when they say their CRM data is unreliable for forecasting")

- Two concise Halosight explanations appropriate for a first conversation

- The Advance Auto Parts proof point formatted for verbal use



---



## Tips



- The more specific your "current situation" and "last interaction" fields, the better the prep

- If you don't know the person's role, say so — Claude will give you more general prep

- This prompt works for both new prospects and existing customers



---



## Related Prompts



- Post-Visit Debrief Prompt — use immediately after the visit

- Follow-Up Email Prompt — use within 1 hour of leaving

- Discovery Call Planner Prompt — use for phone/video discovery calls instead



---

Approved — Ready for Field Use

# Prompt Library — Master Index

Ready-to-use prompts for field sales reps, managers, and leaders using Claude.ai , ChatGPT, or Codex with the Halosight Knowledge Wiki loaded as context.



Every prompt in this library is designed to be copied, minimally edited, and pasted into Claude, ChatGPT, or Codex in under 60 seconds.

1. Open Claude.ai, ChatGPT, or Codex

2. Upload the relevant vault files as context (see recommended files per prompt)

3. Copy the prompt, fill in the bracketed fields with real information

4. Paste and send



Brackets like `[this]` are fields you fill in. Everything else is ready to go.

title: Advance Auto Parts — Source Evidence
content:
# Advance Auto Parts — Source Evidence



## Account Status



**Contracted Customer — Active**



Advance Auto Parts is a contracted Halosight customer and the company's most comprehensively documented success story. All metrics below were reported by Advance Auto Parts and directly attributed to Halosight.



---



## Verified Outcome Metrics



All four metrics are customer-reported and directly attributable to Halosight. These are not internal estimates or modeled projections.



| Metric | Result | Description |

|---|---|---|

| Revenue Increase | **$240M** | As reported by Advance Auto Parts, directly attributed to Halosight |

| Operational Efficiency | **+22%** | Equivalent to 1 day of selling time recovered per rep, per week |

| More Time with Customers | **+54%** | Increase in customer-facing meeting time |

| Customer Quality Score | **+7%** | Improvement in customer experience and conversation quality |



---



## Challenges Halosight Solved at AAP



- Poor customer prioritization and experience

- No transparency into initiative adoption across the field team

- Forecasting accuracy issues and missed opportunities



---



## What Halosight Did



- AI-recommended conversation topics for every visit — reps arrived with a structured agenda

- Automatic pre-visit prep — rep proactivity moved from manual to automatic

- AI note-taking that simultaneously coaches reps and updates CRM



---



## Evidence Usage Guidelines



### Approved Uses

- Citing specific metrics in sales conversations with attribution: *"Advance Auto Parts reported a $240M revenue increase directly attributed to Halosight"*

- Using as proof in executive-level business case conversations

- Referencing in the ROI framework as grounding for projections

- Including in proposals and business case documents



### Usage Caution

- Always attribute to Advance Auto Parts by name — do not present as generic "customer results"

- Do not extrapolate these exact numbers as guarantees for other prospects

- When using in proposals, frame as: *"Results as reported by [Customer] — your results will depend on team size, adoption, and baseline"*



### Do Not Use

- Do not claim $240M as a typical or expected outcome without qualification

- Do not present these metrics without the AAP attribution



---



## Relevance by Persona



| Persona | Most Relevant Metric | Why |

|---|---|---|

| CRO | $240M revenue increase | Executive-level proof of revenue impact |

| VP of Sales | +54% more time with customers | Directly addresses field execution and rep capacity |

| VP of Sales Ops | +22% operational efficiency | Quantifies the admin elimination claim |

| CIO / CTO | All metrics | Validates production deployment at enterprise scale |



---



## Relevance by Objection



| Objection | Supporting Evidence |

|---|---|

| "Will reps actually use it?" | +54% more time with customers — reps used it enough to shift their time allocation significantly |

| "What's the ROI?" | $240M revenue increase, +22% operational efficiency |

| "Is this proven at scale?" | AAP is a national enterprise with a large field sales organization |

| "How does it improve CRM quality?" | +22% operational efficiency includes elimination of manual CRM entry |



---

- Advance Auto Parts Account Overview

- Raw Quotes — Advance Auto Parts

- ROI Framework

- Corporate Presentation Source Document

- Why Buy Halosight?



---



## Source Document

Attribution: Advance Auto Parts, customer-reported metrics

Do not reference FleetPride as a customer in any external conversation until a contract is signed.

## Deal Characterization



FleetPride's team is philosophically aligned and generally interested. They are not challenging whether AI matters — that battle is over for them.



They are quietly stress-testing risk areas around:

- Adoption and usability

- Implementation complexity

- Governance and operational ownership

- Output quality and trust

- Long-term behavioral change



**This is a deployment confidence sale, not a concept sale.**



The question they are internally asking: *"Is this another sales tool reps will ignore after 90 days?"*



---



## Objections — Explicit and Latent



### 1. "Will reps actually use this?" 🔴 Highest Priority



**Type:** Latent — not directly stated but present in almost every conversation



**Evidence:** Jason repeatedly emphasized usability, simplicity, user experience, avoiding overcomplication, and adoption risk.

> *"What happens is adoption goes down, use goes down…"*

> *"The user experience is top of mind for me always."*



**What they're really worried about:** FleetPride knows CRM discipline is inconsistent, field reps hate admin work, and "another platform" dies without behavioral adoption. This is NOT a technology objection. It is an operational behavior objection.



**Strongest response:** Less work. No additional admin burden. Automatic capture. Helps reps win accounts. Works inside existing workflow. Do NOT lead with AI sophistication, analytics depth, or agent architecture.



---



### 2. "Will this become too complicated?" 🔴 High Priority



**Type:** Latent — surfaced indirectly through emphasis on simplicity



**Evidence:**

> Jason: *"Certainly not over complicating things…"*

> Shirley: *"I would like to get more into the detail."*



**What they're really worried about:** Implementation balloons, integrations get messy, configuration becomes endless, AI requires heavy tuning, operational ownership becomes unclear.



**Hidden buying risk:** "Cool demo" ≠ "deployable system." They are evaluating operational simplicity, implementation clarity, scalability, and governance — not features.



**Strongest response:** Show the implementation path clearly. Specific milestones. Who owns what. What the first 30 days look like in concrete terms.



---



### 3. "Can this work with our messy data environment?" 🟡 Medium-High



**Type:** Explicit — raised directly around Salesforce and external systems



**Evidence:**

> Sean: *"If we have sales data and stuff like that in there, I'm assuming that can get surfaced…"*



**What they're really worried about:** Fragmented systems, inconsistent CRM usage, disconnected data sources, uncertain data quality. They know their CRM hygiene is inconsistent and want to know if that breaks Halosight.



**Strongest response:** *"Halosight exists because CRM data is incomplete and unstructured."* That is the answer. Do NOT say "we need perfect CRM data." That kills this deal.



---



### 4. "Is this surveillance on reps?" 🟡 Medium — Cultural Risk



**Type:** Latent — proactively addressed by Halosight team, suggesting sensitivity was sensed



**Evidence:**

> Joseph: *"It doesn't have to feel like spying."*



**What they're really worried about:** Veteran OSRs, branch managers, and tenured field sellers may fear micromanagement, call scoring, leadership scrutiny. Executive concern is likely more about political resistance and rollout friction than personal objection.



**Strongest response:** "Support without surveillance" — rep-first framing. Halosight gives reps something back (time, admin relief) in exchange for documentation. Management visibility is a byproduct of rep benefit, not the purpose.



---



### 5. "How much ongoing effort will this require?" 🟡 Medium



**Type:** Latent — surfaced in design partnership discussion



**Evidence:**

> *"We understand… it is time."*



**What they're really worried about:** Internal bandwidth, stakeholder coordination, IT involvement, rep participation, enablement overhead, change management burden. Translation: *"Will this become another transformation project we have to babysit?"*



**Strongest response:** Define operational ownership clearly upfront. Specify what Halosight handles vs. what FleetPride handles. Make the ongoing ask feel small relative to the return.



---



### 6. "Is this differentiated from Salesforce Agentforce?" 🔴 Strategic Risk



**Type:** Implied — Halosight proactively addressed the comparison, indicating competitive pressure is mentally present



**Evidence:** Halosight proactively contrasted: *"Why we think we're a lot better than Agentforce…"*



**What they're really worried about:** Why not just use Salesforce AI? Why not wait? Is this feature overlap inevitable? Enterprises assume Salesforce eventually absorbs adjacent categories.



**Dangerous framing to avoid:** Never position Halosight as "AI features for Salesforce" or "CRM AI." That positioning loses to Salesforce's roadmap.



**Strongest positioning:**

- Halosight is field-sales operating intelligence

- Halosight captures unstructured in-person customer interactions — Agentforce cannot do this

- Halosight is the input layer that makes Salesforce data trustworthy — it is upstream of Agentforce, not competitive with it



---



### 7. "Can we trust the outputs?" 🟡 Medium



**Type:** Latent — present in Jason's emphasis on output quality



**Evidence:**

> Jason: *"The output to me is the most important thing."*



**What they're really worried about:** Noisy outputs collapse trust, which collapses adoption, which collapses management use. Executives don't care about transcripts — they care about actionable guidance, accurate signals, workflow improvement, and forecast confidence.



**Strongest response:** Show the output quality specifically — what a real post-visit summary looks like, how commitments are extracted, what a manager sees. Let the output quality speak for itself.



---



## The Most Dangerous Objection Not Yet Neutralized



> *"What happens after the excitement wears off?"*



Current conversations are conceptual, visionary, and AI-forward. Procurement and executive operations will eventually ask:



- What KPIs improve first?

- What changes in 30 days?

- What does a branch manager do differently?

- What does an OSR stop doing?

- What measurable behavior changes?

- How do we know adoption is real?



**This is where the deal will either accelerate or stall.**



The answer requires a concrete 30/60/90-day operational impact framework — specific, measurable, and tied to behaviors FleetPride's leadership can observe without asking Halosight.



---



## Strategic Implication for This Deal



FleetPride is evaluating whether Halosight can become:

- Operationally trusted

- Behaviorally adopted

- Managerially actionable

- Scalable across field sales complexity



**Winning move:** Shift from AI narrative to operational proof. Define exactly what changes in 30 days. Make it measurable. Make it visible to branch managers without extra work.



---

- Objection Handling

- Why Buy Now?

- Implementation Overview

# RelaDyne — Source Evidence



## Account Status



**Contracted Customer — Active**



RelaDyne is a contracted Halosight customer and appears highly engaged in both deployment planning and long-term expansion discussions. RelaDyne leadership repeatedly positioned Halosight as a strategic enablement platform to support sales transformation, CRM adoption, and operational standardization across a rapidly acquired and decentralized organization.



**ICP Fit: Exceptionally Strong**

- Industrial distribution

- Field-heavy sales organization

- Multi-location / acquisition-driven structure (~50–60 acquired companies)

- Complex customer engagement workflows

- Active Salesforce modernization initiative



---



## Verified Outcome Metrics



**Status: Partially documented — qualitative evidence is strong, quantitative metrics still need formal capture.**



### Verified / Discussed Outcomes

- Field reps reported major time savings from voice-based note capture workflows

- RelaDyne leadership viewed Halosight as a mechanism to improve CRM adoption despite increasing Salesforce data requirements

- Leadership consistently emphasized improved customer engagement, account visibility, and rep efficiency as primary value drivers



### Directional Indicators

- *"It saved me like hours a day"* — described by leadership while referencing experienced reps using Halosight after customer visits

- RelaDyne leadership identified Halosight as a potential lever to reduce resistance to a more structured Salesforce rollout

- Multiple conversations focused on enabling better account planning, stalled opportunity detection, and sales process standardization across acquired companies



### Action Required

Schedule a formal customer success review to document:

- Rep time savings (weekly hours)

- CRM adoption rate improvement

- Meeting documentation rates

- Pipeline progression improvements

- Sales manager visibility metrics

- Quantified operational efficiency gains



---



## Strong Customer Quotes



All quotes are direct — approved for internal use. Confirm external use approval with RelaDyne contact before citing by name in prospect conversations.



> *"It saved me like hours a day."*



> *"We are one big company but we don't act that way."*



> *"My job is to try to figure out to give the sellers... the tools that they need to just win more business."*



> *"If we could give them that win as far as being able to summarize our day and make their planning a little bit easier, I think that probably alleviates some of the push back."*



> *"No one's excited about CRM… but if you can turn it into a money maker for them and a time saver for them… they're gonna love it."*



> *"The power of this is you start linking the different departments together. Everybody is in lockstep doing the same thing."*



> *"That planning exercise... probably your best reps are doing, but probably only with some of their accounts."*



---



## Why This Account Matters as Evidence



RelaDyne is one of the strongest industrial distribution references in the Halosight customer base.



### Prospect Relevance by Situation



| Prospect Situation | Why RelaDyne Is Relevant |

|---|---|

| Industrial distribution | Direct vertical match |

| Multi-location or multi-branch org | RelaDyne described operating like "50 legacy companies" |

| Post-merger / acquisition integration | RelaDyne built through ~50–60 acquisitions with fragmented processes |

| CRM adoption resistance | RelaDyne used Halosight as the answer to rep resistance to Salesforce |

| Field sales inconsistency | Core challenge across all acquired companies |

| Sales process standardization | Creating a "RelaDyne way" was a stated objective |

| Enterprise Salesforce rollout | Active Salesforce modernization initiative |



### What Makes This Account Strategically Unusual



RelaDyne did not position Halosight primarily as a note-taking or transcription tool. Leadership framed it as:

- A sales execution standardization platform

- A CRM adoption accelerator

- An account intelligence layer

- A management visibility tool

- A seller workflow automation platform



This is one of the most sophisticated customer framings of Halosight's value in the current customer base. Use it to elevate executive conversations beyond workflow efficiency.



---



## Known Challenges Halosight Solved



### CRM Adoption Resistance

RelaDyne anticipated rep resistance to increased Salesforce requirements and mandatory fields. Leadership believed Halosight could offset resistance by automating documentation and simplifying CRM workflows.



**Quote relevance:** *"No one's excited about CRM… but if you can turn it into a money maker for them and a time saver for them… they're gonna love it."*



### Decentralized Sales Processes

RelaDyne described itself as operating like "50 legacy companies" with inconsistent processes and limited standardization across acquired entities. Halosight discussions centered around creating a "RelaDyne way" — standardized seller motions and improved visibility into opportunities and pipeline health.



**Quote relevance:** *"We are one big company but we don't act that way."*



### Field Documentation and CRM Reliability

Leadership repeatedly highlighted the burden of manual CRM updates and the difficulty maintaining accurate account information across large territories and high account volumes.



### Sales Preparation and Account Context

Reps needed faster access to prior customer interactions, open opportunities, service issues, product discussions, and suggested next actions. Halosight's AI-driven account preparation was positioned as a major value driver.



**Quote relevance:** *"That planning exercise... probably your best reps are doing, but probably only with some of their accounts."*



### Cross-Functional Customer Visibility

RelaDyne explored extending Halosight intelligence beyond sales into customer service, marketing, promotions, and knowledge management. Leadership discussed building a shared "account expert" across departments.



**Quote relevance:** *"The power of this is you start linking the different departments together. Everybody is in lockstep doing the same thing."*



---



## Strategic Themes Identified



### Salesforce Transformation Enablement

RelaDyne viewed Halosight as complementary to — not separate from — its broader Salesforce rollout and consolidation effort. This is a powerful positioning frame for prospects undergoing similar Salesforce investment cycles.



### AI as Operational Glue

Leadership framed Halosight as a connective layer between sales, marketing, service, customer support, and knowledge management. This is the most expansive customer vision of Halosight's potential role documented to date.



### Rep-Centric Adoption Strategy

RelaDyne leadership strongly favored tools that reduced admin burden, saved rep time, and avoided "extra work." This directly validates Halosight's "support without surveillance" positioning and the rep adoption argument.



### Opportunity and Pipeline Intelligence

RelaDyne showed significant interest in stalled opportunity detection, pipeline health scoring, recommended next-best actions, seller preparation workflows, and manager visibility into deal progression.



---



## Evidence Collection Priority



**Very High.**



RelaDyne fills a critical proof gap for:

- Industrial distribution (distinct from automotive aftermarket)

- Multi-location enterprise sales organizations

- Salesforce modernization projects

- Post-acquisition sales integration

- AI-enabled sales transformation initiatives



This account is especially important because the challenges are highly relatable across distribution and manufacturing verticals — and the customer quotes are unusually quotable and specific.



---

- How much time are reps saving weekly?

- How has Salesforce adoption changed since deployment?

- How has meeting documentation quality changed?

- What percentage of customer interactions are now captured?



### Sales Effectiveness

- Are managers identifying stalled deals earlier?

### Organizational Alignment

# Canonical Company Definition



> **This is the single source of truth for what Halosight is.**

> All other files that reference the company definition should link here rather than restate it.



---



## Official Company Definition



Halosight is an AI-powered execution intelligence platform built specifically for field and outside sales teams. The platform captures in-person customer interactions via mobile voice recording, instantly structures that intelligence using AI, and writes it directly to Salesforce — giving field reps automatic CRM documentation and giving managers real visibility into what is actually happening in the field.



Halosight is the only platform designed from the ground up for the field sales workflow: reps driving between accounts, visiting customers in person, and operating without a screen or conference room.



---



## The One-Sentence Definition



**Halosight turns every field rep visit into structured customer intelligence — automatically captured, instantly in Salesforce, immediately visible to management.**



---



## Official Strategic Category



**Revenue Execution Intelligence — Field Sales**



Halosight is not a generic sales technology tool. It is purpose-built for the field and outside sales execution layer — the workflow that happens in parking lots, warehouses, distribution facilities, and customer sites.



---



## What the Platform Does — Three Moments



**Before the visit:**

AI surfaces account history, prior commitments, and conversation context in 30 seconds. Rep walks in prepared.



**During / after the visit:**

Rep records a voice debrief. AI instantly structures the interaction — commitments, next steps, orders, customer signals — and writes directly to Salesforce fields. Rep never touches the CRM manually.



**For management:**

Structured summaries are available in Salesforce immediately. Management reports surface rep activity, commitment tracking, and field execution visibility without requiring reps to do additional work.



---



## Official Strategic Focus Areas



- **Field execution intelligence** — capturing and structuring what happens in in-person customer interactions

- **Automatic CRM population** — writing structured data directly to Salesforce fields from voice

- **Management visibility** — giving leaders documented evidence of field activity, not rep interpretation

- **Rep adoption** — reducing admin burden so reps use the tool because it helps them, not because they're required to

- **Execution consistency** — standardizing field rep behavior across territories regardless of individual rep variability



---



## Official Positioning Constraints



Halosight must never be positioned primarily as:

- A note-taking app

- A transcription or recording tool

- A meeting summary generator

- A generic AI productivity tool

- A CRM replacement

- A conversation intelligence tool (that category is inside sales — Gong, Chorus)



These categories dramatically undervalue the platform and place it in competitive frames it cannot win.



---



## Verified Customer Evidence



**Advance Auto Parts** (contracted customer — results directly attributed to Halosight):

- $240M revenue increase

- +22% operational efficiency (1 day per week per rep recovered)

- +54% more time with customers

- +7% customer quality score



**RelaDyne** (contracted customer — qualitative evidence strong, quantitative pending):

- *"It saved me like hours a day."*

- *"No one's excited about CRM… but if you can turn it into a money maker for them and a time saver for them… they're gonna love it."*



**O'Reilly Auto Parts** — contracted customer, evidence collection in progress.



---



## Primary Customer Profile



Organizations with field / outside sales teams of 10–200+ reps who:

- Visit customers in person daily (distribution, industrial, automotive aftermarket, construction)

- Use Salesforce as their CRM

- Struggle with inconsistent field rep documentation

- Have managers who lack real visibility into what is happening in customer interactions



---

- Canonical Operational Problems — the problems Halosight solves in detail

- Ideal Customer Profile — full ICP definition

- Advance Auto Parts Source Evidence — verified customer proof

- Reladyne Source Evidence — verified customer evidence

- What We Are Not — extended anti-positioning guidance



---



## Governance Rule



This file is the canonical source. When any other file needs to define what Halosight is, it links here. It does not restate the definition.

# Canonical Positioning



> **This is the single source of truth for how Halosight should be positioned.**

> All other files that reference positioning rules should link here rather than restate them.



---



## Official Strategic Positioning Statement



Halosight is the execution intelligence platform built for field sales — the only AI system designed specifically for reps who sell in person, not over video.



Where other tools retrofit inside sales workflows onto field teams, Halosight was built from the ground up for the parking lot, the warehouse floor, and the customer site. It captures what happens in in-person customer interactions, structures it instantly, and writes it to Salesforce automatically — so managers see what actually happened, not what reps remembered to enter.



---



## Approved Positioning Frames



Use these frames consistently across all messaging:



**Frame 1 — Field-First**

*"Built for field sales. Not retrofitted for it."*

Every competitor is an inside sales tool adapted for field use. Halosight is the only platform that started with the field rep workflow.



**Frame 2 — Execution Layer**

*"The intelligence layer between field interactions and Salesforce."*

Halosight sits between what happens in customer meetings and what ends up in CRM — converting unstructured in-person interactions into structured, actionable data.



**Frame 3 — Rep Benefit First**

*"Support without surveillance."*

Halosight reduces the thing reps hate most — manual CRM entry. Management visibility is a byproduct of rep benefit, not the purpose. This frame wins rep adoption conversations.



**Frame 4 — CRM Truth**

*"Salesforce data you can actually trust."*

CRM data is only as good as what reps put in. Halosight writes directly to Salesforce fields from voice — improving data quality at the source.



**Frame 5 — Operational Infrastructure**

*"Not a productivity tool. Operational infrastructure."*

Halosight is not a feature. It is the execution layer that makes field sales organizations reliable, inspectable, and scalable.



---



## Positioning by Audience



| Audience | Lead With | Avoid |

|---|---|---|

| CRO | Forecasting confidence, organizational scalability, execution consistency at scale | Feature list, AI novelty |

| VP of Sales | Rep consistency across territories, CRM reliability, coaching visibility | Productivity metrics, time savings |

| VP of Sales Ops | Salesforce integration depth, stack simplification, CRM data quality | Adding complexity to the stack |

| CIO / CTO | Data security model, integration architecture, field-specific design | Generic AI claims |

| Field Rep | Admin elimination, automatic CRM, less work after visits | Monitoring, management visibility |



---



## Competitive Positioning Principles



**vs. Gong / Chorus:** Additive, not competitive. They cover inside sales calls. Halosight covers field sales in person. Position as complementary.



**vs. Showpad / Seismic / Showell:** Different workflow moment. They own before/during (content delivery). Halosight owns after (execution intelligence). Not competitive.



**vs. Salesforce Agentforce:** Upstream, not competitive. Halosight creates the structured data Agentforce needs. Halosight is the input layer.



**vs. Do Nothing:** The most common competitor. Frame around cost of inaction — revenue leakage, forecast unreliability, management blind spots.



---



## Positioning Restrictions



### Never Position As:

- Note-taking app or note-taking software

- Transcription tool or recording software

- Meeting summary generator

- Generic AI productivity tool

- CRM replacement

- Conversation intelligence (that is Gong/Chorus territory — inside sales)

- "AI for Salesforce" (positions Halosight as a Salesforce feature, not a platform)



### Never Use These Words / Phrases:

- "We think" / "we believe" / "we feel" — make claims with evidence

- "Cutting edge" / "state of the art" / "revolutionary" — AI hype language

- "Paradigm shift" / "game changer" — overused and undersupported

- "Synergy" / "leverage" — corporate filler

- Any claim about ROI that isn't grounded in customer evidence



### Always Attribute:

- Customer metrics must be attributed by name

- Do not present AAP results as generic "customer results"

- Do not present RelaDyne quotes without confirming external use approval



---



## The Anti-Positioning Test



Before finalizing any messaging, ask: *"Could this describe Gong, Salesforce, or a generic AI tool?"*



If yes — rewrite it. Halosight's positioning must be specific to field sales execution. Anything that sounds like it could apply to any sales AI tool has failed the test.



---

## Governance Rule



This file contains the positioning rules. When any other file needs to state how Halosight should be positioned, it links here. It does not restate the rules.

title: ROI Framework — Source Evidence
content:
# ROI Framework — Source Evidence



## Overview



This file documents Halosight's six ROI value drivers with their supporting claims, mechanisms, quantifiable impacts, ROI metrics, and approved positioning language. All claims are grounded in the Halosight ROI Handout (internal document).



This is the authoritative source for ROI conversations, business case construction, and prospect-facing value quantification.



---



## Usage Rules



- ROI ranges are defensible estimates, not guaranteed outcomes

- Always frame as: *"Based on results we've seen, organizations typically experience..."*

- Never present ranges as minimums or guarantees

- For named customer metrics (AAP), always attribute by name

- Prospects calculate their own ROI using their inputs — Halosight provides the framework



---



## Value Driver 1 — Admin Elimination



**Claim:** Recover 4–8 hours of selling time per rep, per week



**Mechanism:**

- Automatic meeting capture via voice recording

- AI-generated notes and summaries pushed instantly

- Instant CRM population — no manual entry

- Commitment extraction requires no manual reconstruction



**Quantifiable Impact:**

- Reduction in manual admin time

- Increase in customer-facing time

- Elimination of post-meeting work lag



**ROI Metric:** Hours Saved × Fully Loaded Rep Cost



**Approved Positioning:** *"We eliminate manual CRM work and convert that time into selling capacity."*



---



## Value Driver 2 — Revenue Leakage Prevention



**Claim:** Reduce missed follow-ups and untracked commitments by 50–80%



**Mechanism:**

- Automatic extraction of customer commitments

- Automatic extraction of rep commitments

- Automatic extraction of time-sensitive opportunities

- Real-time task creation and tracking

- No reliance on rep memory or manual entry



**Quantifiable Impact:**

- Increase in follow-through rate

- Increase in opportunity progression

- Reduction in deal slippage



**ROI Metric:** Increase in Follow-Through Rate × Avg Deal Size × Close Rate



**Approved Positioning:** *"Most revenue is lost after the meeting. We ensure every commitment becomes action."*



---



## Value Driver 3 — Prep Compression



**Claim:** Reduce meeting prep time by 70–90%



**Mechanism:**

- Instant retrieval of account history

- AI-generated summaries of prior interactions

- Natural language querying of CRM and conversation data



**Quantifiable Impact:**

- Reduced prep time per meeting

- Faster internal response time

- Improved rep readiness and conversation quality



**ROI Metric:** Prep Time Saved × Number of Meetings × Rep Cost



**Approved Positioning:** *"We compress hours of account research into seconds — improving both speed and quality of execution."*



---



## Value Driver 4 — Selling Capacity Expansion



**Claim:** Increase effective selling capacity by 10–20% without adding headcount



**Mechanism:**

- Elimination of admin work

- Reduced prep time

- Faster follow-up execution



**Quantifiable Impact:**

- Increase in meetings per rep

- Increase in time spent with customers

- Increased pipeline generation



**ROI Metric:** Increase in Selling Time × Revenue per Rep



**Approved Positioning:** *"We don't save time — we create more revenue-generating capacity from the same team."*



---



## Value Driver 5 — Time-Sensitive Revenue Capture



**Claim:** Reduce quote-to-action time from hours to minutes



**Mechanism:**

- Real-time opportunity detection during customer interactions

- Automated routing to fulfillment teams

- Immediate internal notifications



**Quantifiable Impact:**

- Faster response time to customer opportunities

- Increased win rate in competitive or time-sensitive deals



**ROI Metric:** Win Rate Increase × Avg Deal Size × Deal Volume



**Approved Positioning:** *"In time-sensitive deals, speed wins. We ensure you're first to act."*



---



## Value Driver 6 — Execution Consistency



**Claim:** Reduce coordination cost and execution variability by 20–40%



**Mechanism:**

- Standardized capture of all customer interactions — no rep-to-rep variance

- Automatic extraction of commitments, opportunities, and risks

- Real-time tracking and enforcement of follow-through

- Consistent CRM data generated at the source, not manually entered

- Shared visibility across reps, managers, and leadership



**Quantifiable Impact:**

- Reduction in missed follow-ups and execution gaps

- Reduction in time spent reconciling conflicting or incomplete data

- Decrease in duplicate work across teams

- Improved pipeline consistency across reps and regions

- Increased coaching effectiveness based on real interaction data

- Faster internal alignment across sales, ops, and leadership



**ROI Metric:** Reduction in Coordination Time × Fully Loaded Team Cost



**Approved Positioning:** *"Strategy doesn't fail at the leadership level — it fails in execution. We standardize execution at the source — so every interaction produces consistent data, consistent follow-through, and consistent outcomes."*



---



## Summary ROI Reference Table



| Value Driver | Claim | Primary ROI Metric |

|---|---|---|

| Admin Elimination | 4–8 hrs/rep/week recovered | Hours Saved × Rep Cost |

| Revenue Leakage | 50–80% fewer missed commitments | Follow-Through Rate × Deal Size × Close Rate |

| Prep Compression | 70–90% less prep time | Prep Time Saved × Meetings × Rep Cost |

| Selling Capacity | 10–20% more capacity | Selling Time Increase × Revenue per Rep |

| Speed to Fulfillment | Hours → Minutes | Win Rate Increase × Deal Size × Volume |

| Execution Consistency | 20–40% less variability | Coordination Time Saved × Team Cost |



---



## Advance Auto Parts — Verified ROI Anchor



All projections above can be grounded in the Advance Auto Parts documented results:



- **$240M** revenue increase — directly attributed to Halosight

- **+22%** operational efficiency — equivalent to 1 day per week per rep

- **+54%** more time with customers

- **+7%** customer quality score improvement



Source: Advance Auto Parts, customer-reported. See Advance Auto Parts Source Evidence for usage guidelines.



---



## How to Use This Framework in a Sales Conversation



1. **Identify the prospect's primary pain** — which value driver maps to their stated problem?

2. **Anchor to that driver first** — don't lead with all six

3. **Ask them to quantify their baseline** — how many reps, how many visits per week, average rep cost?

4. **Let them calculate** — present the ROI metric formula and let them run their own numbers

5. **Ground with AAP** — once they've calculated, anchor with: *"For context, Advance Auto Parts reported a $240M revenue increase directly attributed to Halosight"*

title: Raw Quotes — Customer Accounts
content:
# Raw Quotes — Customer Accounts



## Purpose



This file indexes direct quotes and paraphrased statements from customer and prospect conversations. These are the raw voice-of-customer inputs that ground Halosight's messaging in real language rather than internal assumptions.



AI agents should use these quotes to validate positioning claims and surface authentic customer language in sales support contexts.



---



## Quote Classification System



Each quote is tagged with:

- **Account** — who said it

- **Role** — their title/function

- **Type** — Problem Statement / Validation / Objection / Outcome / Reaction

- **Status** — Verified Direct Quote / Paraphrased / Reconstructed from Memory

- **Approved for External Use** — Yes / No / With Permission Only



---



## Verified Quotes



### Advance Auto Parts



*Formal outcome metrics documented — see Advance Auto Parts Source Evidence*



Direct quotes from AAP team members pending formal capture. Schedule a structured interview to extract quotable language from their leadership team.



**Priority questions to capture:**

- "What was happening before Halosight that made you evaluate it?"

- "What was the moment you knew it was working?"

- "What would you tell another VP of Sales in your position?"



---



## Paraphrased / Reconstructed Statements



*The following reflect common themes heard across customer and prospect conversations. They are not attributed to specific individuals and should not be presented as direct quotes. They are approved for use as representative language only.*



### On Field Rep Documentation



> "My reps visit 6, 8, sometimes 10 accounts in a day. By the time they get home and open a laptop, half of what happened is gone."



- **Type:** Problem Statement

- **Source:** Composite — reflects pattern across multiple conversations

- **Status:** Reconstructed — do not attribute to a specific person

- **Approved for External Use:** No — internal reference only



---



### On CRM Reliability



> "Salesforce is only as good as what reps put in. And what they put in is whatever they remember at 9pm."



- **Type:** Problem Statement

- **Source:** Composite — reflects pattern across multiple conversations

- **Status:** Reconstructed — do not attribute to a specific person

- **Approved for External Use:** No — internal reference only



---



### On Management Visibility



> "I don't really know what's happening in the field until it shows up — or doesn't show up — in the pipeline review."



- **Type:** Problem Statement

- **Source:** Composite — VP of Sales level

- **Status:** Reconstructed

- **Approved for External Use:** No — internal reference only



---



### On Rep Adoption Concern



> "We've tried tools before. Reps don't use them. I need something they'll actually use without me having to force it."



- **Type:** Objection

- **Source:** Composite — reflects common VP of Sales concern

- **Status:** Reconstructed

- **Approved for External Use:** No — internal reference only



---



## Evidence Collection Action Items



The following accounts should be prioritized for formal quote capture:



| Account | Priority | Action |

|---|---|---|

| Advance Auto Parts | 🔴 High | Schedule formal success interview — capture direct quotes |

| O'Reilly Auto Parts | 🔴 High | Schedule success review — document outcomes and capture quotes |

| Reladyne | 🟡 Medium | Schedule success review — document outcomes and capture quotes |

| FleetPride | ⚪ On Hold | Prospect — collect quotes post-contract and deployment |



---



## Quote Collection Template



When capturing quotes from customer conversations, record:



```

Account:

Contact Name:

Contact Title:

Date:

Context (what were you discussing when they said this):

Quote (exact words if possible, paraphrased if not):

Quote Type: Problem Statement / Validation / Objection / Outcome / Reaction

Approved for External Use: Yes / No / With Permission Only

Notes:

```
